channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Villagers are wary of plans to dam a river to ensure Panama Canal's water supply
item.description: EL JOBO, Panama (AP) — A long, wooden boat puttered down the Indio River’s chocolate waters carrying Ana María Antonio and a colleague from the Panama Canal Authority on a mission to hear directly from villagers who could be affected by plans to dam the river.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A look behind the scenes at the National Toy Hall of Fame
item.description: ROCHESTER, N.Y. (AP) — When curators at the National Toy Hall of Fame learned last fall that the Fisher-Price Corn Popper had been voted in as part of the class of 2023, they knew they had some serious work to do.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A man accused of threatening to kill Democratic election officials will likely plead guilty
item.description: A man accused of making repeated threats to kill the top election officials in Colorado and Arizona as well as judges and federal law enforcement agents is expected to plead guilty in federal court on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Slevin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Surfer dies after being struck, impaled by swordfish
item.description: A longtime surfer and travel guide died after being impaled by a swordfish off the coast of Indonesia.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KREX 5

item.title: Growing number of men vs. Diddy lawsuits 'part of industry': Ray J
item.description: "It's a power trip, and it hits hard. I know a few celebs who have been in that crazy situation," Ray J said on "CUOMO.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Want to be a Power Ranger? Original costumes, props up for auction
item.description: It's been over 30 years since the original Mighty Morphin Power Rangers television series aired in the United States, and for the kids who grew up watching the Power Rangers fight evil in Angel Grove, California, their chance to become one of them is now closer than ever.
item.dc:creator: Marco Rosas, Fox 5

item.title: Judge tosses RNC challenge to Michigan voter roll maintenance
item.description: U.S. District Judge Jane Beckering ruled that neither the RNC nor two individual voters who joined the suit had legal standing to bring their case.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Judge tosses RNC challenge to Michigan voter roll maintenance
item.description: U.S. District Judge Jane Beckering ruled that neither the RNC nor two individual voters who joined the suit had legal standing to bring their case.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KRON4

item.title: Iran Revolutionary Guard official charged in plot to kill Iranian American human rights activist in NYC
item.description: U.S. prosecutors have charged a member of the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps of Iran in an assassination plot to kill an Iranian American human rights activist on American soil.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: September Border Patrol encounters lowest since the pandemic
item.description: Fewer migrants were apprehended between ports of entry at the southern border in September than in any month since August of 2020, according to new data released by Customs and Border Protection (CBP).
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Former Abercrombie & Fitch chief Mike Jeffries arrested on federal sex trafficking charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO Mike Jeffries, his romantic partner and a third man were arrested Tuesday on charges of luring men into drug-laced, outlandish and coercive sex parties by dangling the promise of modeling for the retailer's once-defining beefcake ads.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Carolina county surrounding Asheville overcounted Helene deaths by as many as 30, sheriff says
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — The North Carolina county that is home to Asheville overcounted deaths caused by Helene by as many as 30, according to a statement Tuesday from its sheriff’s office and data from the state, significantly reducing the death toll from the historic storm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McDonald's Quarter Pounder burgers linked to E. coli outbreak
item.description: There has been one reported death due to the outbreak, according to the release. No recall has been issued as of Tuesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: LA City Council candidate says 'F--- the police' in leaked audio, prompting blowback from police union
item.description: A candidate for the Los Angeles City Council is under fire for saying, "F--- the police," when asked about her stance on funds for police overtime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dolton mayor Tiffany Henyard dubbed 'America's worst mayor' running for reelection
item.description: The mayor of Dolton, Ill., Tiffany Henyard, is likely the most controversial mayor ever elected in the town of 20,000, facing multiple controversies.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Captain rescued after surviving Hurricane Milton inside cooler: boat owner
item.description: The Coast Guard rescued the captain of the "Capt. Dave" boat following Hurricane Milton. The ship's owner, Steven Hawley, indicated that the captain survived in a cooler box.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump makes more debunked claims about FEMA as he visits storm damaged North Carolina
item.description: Trump was asked whether it was helpful to criticize hurricane relief workers after the Federal Emergency Management Agency recently paused work in the area because of reports they could be targeted by militia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Tracking the Tropics: Hurricane Oscar misses Florida in active October
item.description: Tropical Storm Oscar moves through the central and southeastern Bahamas, The National Hurricane Center said.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Harris speaks about creating economic opportunity for Latino men; Trump airs familiar grievances
item.description: Democratic presidential nominee Kamala Harris says she would work to bring more funds to community banks to help Latino men secure small business loans, while Republican Donald Trump’s economic roundtable aimed at Latino voters devolved Tuesday into a tirade of insults against his opponent.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Asian American boba brand finds opportunity after Simu Liu sparks cultural appropriation debate
item.description: Olivia Chen and Pauline Ang, friends and business partners on boba milk tea brand Twrl, have tried three times to get on “Shark Tank,” the ABC reality show where up-and-coming entrepreneurs try to woo big-name backers.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas man whose execution was halted doesn't appear at state Capitol after subpoena
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A Texas man whose execution was halted and then called to testify at the state Capitol did not show up Monday over growing pushback from state leaders who have blasted lawmakers' last-ditch tactics to subpoena Robert Roberson and spare his life for now.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hezbollah hiding more than $500M in gold, cash under hospital in Lebanon, IDF says
item.description: Israel on Monday said Hezbollah is hiding millions in gold and cash under a Lebanese hospital but will not target the building for attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Eyewitness video captures frantic efforts to save lives after deadly collapse of dock walkway
item.description: SAVANNAH, Ga. (AP) — The metal gangway where dozens of people waited to board a ferry boat made a loud, creaking noise before snapping in the middle amid panicked cries from those sent plunging into the water.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM and EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Starbucks releasing 'Wicked' drinks inspired by the upcoming film
item.description: Starbucks is releasing a pair of limited-edition drinks inspired by two main characters of the upcoming film, "Wicked.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KRON4

item.title: McDonald's says it doesn't endorse political candidates after Trump visit
item.description: McDonald's is emphasizing its political neutrality after former President Donald Trump spent some time behind the counter at one of its Pennsylvania locations.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, KRON4

item.title: Central Park Five sue Donald Trump for jogger case remarks made at presidential debate
item.description: With Election Day two weeks away, the group accused the former president of making “false and defamatory statements.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Zenni Optical offering free glasses for those affected by Hurricane Helene, Milton
item.description: Zenni Optical, a Nothern California company that sells prescription glasses online, is offering free glasses for those impacted by Hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Prosecutors: Ex-officer's gunshots put people in danger the night of deadly Breonna Taylor raid
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — The 10 shots Brett Hankison fired into Breonna Taylor 's apartment the night she was killed in a police raid put Taylor, her neighbors and even one of Hankison’s fellow Louisville police officers in danger, federal prosecutors argued Monday.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: PHOTOS: White House public tour gets makeover with 'reimagined' visitor experience
item.description: The White House public tour is getting an extreme makeover, with new digital exhibits, an expanded route and more access to rooms poised to be part of the "reimagined" experience for visitors.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WGN 9

item.title: Paul Di'Anno, former Iron Maiden lead singer, dies at 66
item.description: Paul Andrews, better known by his stage name, Paul Di'Anno of Iron Maiden fame has died at the age of 66.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Yankees and Dodgers meet in World Series for 12th time in matchup of Broadway and Hollywood
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Broadway vs. Hollywood. Subway vs. Freeway. Judge vs. Ohtani. New York neighbors who became cross-country rivals, the Yankees and Dodgers renew their starry struggle in the World Series for the first time in 43 years.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Oscar brings winds, heavy rains to eastern Cuba after striking the Bahamas
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Tropical Storm Oscar brought heavy rains and winds to Cuba, an island already beleaguered by a massive power outage, late Sunday after brushing the Bahamas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Battleground Florida: FEMA funds
item.description: Tampa Bay is working to recover from two recent back-to-back hurricanes that impacted the state weeks away from the presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cooper scores in his Bills debut and Allen shakes off a sluggish start in 34-10 rout of Titans
item.description: It didn’t take Amari Cooper long to get the hang of Josh Allen and the Buffalo Bills' offense — even when the quarterback was changing plays at the line of scrimmage.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lions top previously unbeaten Vikings 31-29 on late FG to cap NFC North thriller
item.description: The Detroit Lions became division champions for the first time in 30 years late last season with a clinching win at Minnesota. They departed this time with another memorable and resilient performance — plus a big boost in their pursuit of another NFC North title.
item.dc:creator: DAVE CAMPBELL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans head to court in expanding battle about overseas ballots
item.description: Overseas voting has become the latest battlefront in Republicans’ legal challenges leading up to the election.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: California woman slashed dog's throat, left it alive in trash: deputies
item.description: The woman "felt her life would be easier if she only had to care for one dog and cut one of the dog’s throats," say officials.
item.dc:creator: Veronica Catlin, WFLA, NBC
